overview: From 2026-06-18 to 2026-06-19, elizaos/eliza had 103 new PRs (98 merged), 1 new issues, and 12 active contributors.

topIssues.title: VIEWS action hijacks text-connector (Discord/Matrix) turns → desktop-only nav, no user-visible reply
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

On a **text connector** (Discord / Matrix / Telegram), the planner can route a normal user question to the desktop-only **`VIEWS`** action, which produces a desktop-UI navigation (e.g. "Placed Help on the right.") and **no user-visible reply on the text channel**. The user asks a question and gets nothing back.

`VIEWS` read-modes are exposed and selectable on every connector unconditionally — there is no gate on whether the connector actually surfaces views to the user who sent the message. Develop has recently leaned into view-switching (`426828c539 feat(views): strengthen view-switching encouragement + detection`), which increases how often the planner picks `VIEWS` for ambiguous prompts, making this user-visible.

## Repro (live, Discord connector)

Prompt to the bot in a Discord channel:

> `@bot hey — quick check, you alive? what connectors are you on right now?`

The turn routes to `VIEWS {action: "list"}`. Trajectory tool stage:

```json
{"name":"VIEWS","args":{"action":"list"},
 "result":{"success":true,"userFacingText":"Placed Help on the right.","verifiedUserFacing":true,
   "data":{"actionName":"VIEWS","viewId":"help","action":"split-view"}},
 "toolCallsExecuted":1,"toolCallFailures":0}
```

Planner action surface for the turn (`tierAParents:["VIEWS"]`, `REPLY` omitted):

```
mode=tiered candidateActions=["VIEWS"] tierAParents=["VIEWS"]
omittedParentNamesPreview=[...,"REPLY",...]
```

Result on Discord: **no message is sent** — `VIEWS` only navigates the desktop dashboard, which a Discord-only user never sees.

Contrast: an unambiguous text question (`"what is 2+2, are you online?"`) correctly replies via the fast path (`"Two plus two is four, and yes I'm online."`), so the pipeline is healthy — this is specifically about `VIEWS` being offered/selected on text connectors.

## Root cause

`plugins/plugin-app-control/src/actions/views.ts` — read-mode `validate()` returns `true` unconditionally:

```ts
// Read modes are visible to all users.
return true;
```

There is no check for whether the message's connector has a live view surface for that user. So `VIEWS` is always a planner candidate, even on a pure text deployment where the action can only ever be a no-op for the asker.

## Why it matters

`VIEWS` selected as the **sole/terminal** action on a text-connector turn is a silent non-answer. The runtime records `verifiedUserFacing: true` (because the desktop got a view), so it looks like a successful turn while the chat user received nothing.

## Nuance (why this isn't a one-line gate)

There's a legitimate desktop-companion case: an owner chatting via Discord may *also* have the desktop dashboard open, so navigating their view is meaningful. The framework can't easily know "is this user looking at a view surface right now." So the fix isn't simply `return false` for text connectors.

## Proposed approaches (for discussion)

1. **Gate on a real view-surface signal** — `VIEWS.validate()` returns `false` unless the runtime can confirm an attached view surface for the session (dashboard/desktop client connected), rather than assuming one exists.
2. **Never let `VIEWS` be terminal on a text connector** — when the chosen action is `VIEWS` and the message arrived via a text connector, require it to be paired with a `REPLY` (the view nav becomes a side effect, the text answer is still delivered).
3. **Connector-capability tiering** — exclude desktop-only actions (`VIEWS`, `CLOSE_VIEW`, `CLOSE_ALL_VIEWS`, `HOMESCREEN`) from the planner action surface for connectors that declare no view capability.

Approach 2 is the most behavior-preserving (keeps view-switching where a surface exists, guarantees the chat user always gets an answer). Happy to implement whichever direction maintainers prefer.

## Environment

- Live self-hosted agent on `develop` @ `36f7877267`, Discord + Matrix connectors, `plugin-app-control` loaded.
- Reproduced 2026-06-18.

topPRs.title: Integrate milady/ios-fused-voice-slice → develop (iOS fused voice + CoreML Kokoro + on-device model-grind, plus app-control/cloud/orchestrator/views work)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Integration PR for the active `milady/ios-fused-voice-slice` working branch into `develop` (57 commits ahead; 310 behind at open — rebase/merge as the maintainers prefer).

## Highlights (this session)
- **CoreML Kokoro E2E converter** (`packages/training/scripts/kokoro/coreml/`): fused Kokoro-82M → single CoreML graph matching the iOS `KokoroCoreMlModel` contract (in-graph fixed-bucket alignment, masked AdaIN, injected hn-NSF phase, int32 length). Torch module validated **0.99 spectral** vs real Kokoro; CoreML conversion is structurally correct with a documented vocoder `F.interpolate`/iSTFT fidelity gap (WIP) → **not staged**; iOS uses the fused GGUF Kokoro path until parity lands.
- **On-device grind-all-models telemetry test** (`plugins/plugin-capacitor-bridge/src/ios/model-grind.ts` + `/api/dev/model-grind` route + env-gated auto-run): grinds text(+MTP) / TTS (RTF) / TTS→ASR (WER) round-trip with per-model load+infer ms and peak RSS. 12 unit tests.
- **Memory + e2e latency review** (`plugins/plugin-local-inference/docs/memory-and-e2e-latency-review.md`): code-grounded voice latency waterfall, eviction/streaming inefficiency inventory, per-RAM-tier (8→128 GB) co-residency policy, ranked optimizations.

## Other workstreams on this branch (other agents)
app-control verification (VIEWS/HOMESCREEN real outcomes), cloud monetization/charging proof, orchestrator multi-account coding sub-agents, views hero images + route coverage, plugin live-loading.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

When a view's search box moved to the floating chat, **nothing told the user where search went**. Worse: w

Fixes verified, user-visible bugs in the Telegram **bot** (telegraf) connector. The inbound polling path is already resilient (telegraf honors `retry_after`/5xx on receive) and is left untouched; this hardens the outbound + parsing

completedItems.title: test(cloud-api): run unit tests process-per-file to kill mock.module cross-contamination
completedItems.body:
## The flake
The cloud-api unit suite is order-dependent: bun evaluates every file's top-level `mock.module` at **collection time** in one shared process, and those overrides are process-global with no per-file teardown. **10 of 24 files**

completedItems.title: fix(cloud): persist shared-runtime chat history in Postgres (durable, cache-independent)
completedItems.body: Prod ship of #8575 (also on develop). Shared agents stored chat history only in the request cache, which is disabled on the prod Worker (`CACHE_ENABLED=false`) → history never persisted + no cross-turn memory. Moves it to an isolated Postgr

completedItems.title: fix(cloud): persist shared-runtime chat history in Postgres (durable, cache-independent)
completedItems.body:
## Shared agents had no durable chat history

Shared (Tier-0) agents stored conversation history **only** in the request cache, which is **disabled on the prod Worker** (`CACHE_ENABLED=false`, no KV backend) → `cache.set` was a silent no-op
